## Findings
- [ ] **[WARN][Coverage] Patch coverage of unreleased diff is 60.6%**
  - **Status quo:** Only 154 of 254 changed production lines in v0.1.6..HEAD are covered. Several SSRF-hardening and pipeline branches are untested (see `.github/coverage/2026-06-23T11-08-55Z.md`).
  - **Likely cause:** Defensive branches added in #120 and #126 are not fully exercised by the new regression tests.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add targeted tests for the uncovered ranges before the next release, or accept as a warn-only finding because overall coverage rose 13.0 points.
  - **Handoff:** `/test-coverage` → `/02-gh-plan --from-coverage .github/coverage/2026-06-23T11-08-55Z.md` → `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[WARN][Docs] README does not explicitly document flag/config precedence**
  - **Status quo:** #120 added the behavior that CLI flags (`--no-cache`, `--cache-ttl`, `--concurrency`) override config-file/env values when supplied. The README Configuration section lists env vars but does not state the precedence rule.
  - **Likely cause:** README last updated for v0.1.0; new CLI/config integration in #120 was not reflected.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add one sentence to the Configuration section noting that explicit flags take precedence over config/env values.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` or `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[WARN][Lint] Repository is not gofmt-clean**
  - **Status quo:** `make lint` runs `go fmt ./...` and reformats 13 files. CI does not enforce `gofmt`, so this is not a hard gate.
  - **Likely cause:** Recent commits added code that is functionally correct but not gofmt-formatted.
  - **Proposed solution:** Run `go fmt ./...` in a dedicated cleanup commit or include the formatting fixes in the release commit.
  - **Handoff:** `/commit` or manual `go fmt ./...`
